did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Assistant Dean, School of Hotel Administration.

Paul Laurence Gaurnier was born in 1923, received his degree from Cornell University in 1950, and served in the United States Army in administrative, managerial and operations positions. In 1967, he became Assistant Dean. He was responsible for the Cornell Hotel and Restaurant Administration Summer School and was the project director of the school's adjunct training program in Puerto Rico.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; files on the Puerto Rico hotel school; seminar information for various groups in the U.S. and worldwide; course information for Principles of Management, Staff Planning and Operations Analysis, Case Studies in the Hospitality Industry, and other courses; consultant reports by Gaurnier, primarily for hotels, but including some for the World Bank; and files concerning other members of the staff and faculty of the Hotel School and/or Hotel School activities.

controlaccess.head: Names:
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. School of Hotel Administration.

scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Paul L. Gaurnier. Papers, #28-2-2323. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
